en_title: Gen Atlas Creator Fumito Ueda on Creating Emotional Masterpieces: ‘I Do Think About Leaving a Mark in People’s Hearts’
cn_title: 上田文人谈《Gen Atlas》：想在玩家心中留下印记
subtitle: 第N次等待
opus_summary: 上田文人新作《Gen Atlas》从奇幻转向科幻，玩家可操控巨型机器人同伴，战斗更强。作品延续孤独与羁绊主题，无发售日，上田希望玩家十年后仍铭记那个世界。

paragraphs.en: There's a unique sense of intrigue that surrounds every new Fumito Ueda project. Familiar, yet exciting. A promise of a connection between character and player that few others can forge. But the Ico , Shadow of the Colossus , and The Last Guardian creator is moving away from his fantasy worlds with Gen Atlas , shifting to a land of sci-fi where giant robots lie strewn across its shores, waiting to be awoken. That quiet image of towering metal creatures served as the spark for this new project.
paragraphs.cn: 上田文人的每一个新项目都笼罩着一种独特的好奇心。熟悉，却又激动人心。那是他许下的承诺，要在角色与玩家之间建立起一种其他游戏鲜能铸就的羁绊。但这位《ICO》《旺达与巨像》和《最后的守护者》的缔造者，正在通过《Gen Atlas》离开他的奇幻世界，转向一片科幻之地——海岸边散落着巨型机器人，等待着被唤醒。那些高耸金属造物的静谧画面，正是这个新项目的火花。

paragraphs.en: “I knew I wanted to feature giant robots in the game,” Ueda told me in an interview at last week’s Summer Game Fest. “But the slightly longer answer is that there was an image in my head that really stuck, and I couldn't let go of that visual that I had. The robot's head is detached, and it's carrying its own head underneath the arm. I knew that I wanted that somehow to be in the game, and then it kind of expanded from that.”
paragraphs.cn: 「我知道自己想在游戏里放巨型机器人，」上田在上周的夏日游戏节采访中告诉我。「但稍微展开来说，是因为我脑海中有一幅挥之不去的画面，我放不下那个视觉印象。机器人的头身分离，它用自己的手臂夹着脑袋。我知道无论如何都想把它放进游戏里，然后整个构想就从这里扩展开来。」

paragraphs.en: What exactly we’ll be doing with these titanium torsos and steel skulls remains much of a mystery. But after viewing the extended version of Gen Atlas’ SGF trailer , which showcases a little extra gameplay, I can make some inferences. It seems to me as if it’s an evolution of The Last Guardian’s companion concept, just with one huge difference: this time, the oversized companion itself will be controllable, as well as the human protagonist. Perhaps Ueda has taken on feedback from players that this is a desirable option that has never been the case in any of his games to date.
paragraphs.cn: 至于玩家究竟会如何与这些钛合金身躯和钢铁头颅互动，目前仍是个谜。不过，在看过《Gen Atlas》夏日游戏节预告片的加长版后——其中展示了更多实机画面——我已经有了一些推测。在我看来，这似乎是对《最后的守护者》伙伴系统的一次进化，但有一个巨大的不同：这一次，玩家不仅能操控人类主角，似乎还能直接操控那位体型庞大的伙伴。或许上田文人听取了玩家反馈，意识到大家一直希望拥有这项功能，而他此前的作品从未提供过这种玩法。

paragraphs.en: Combat will also feature more heavily – I saw rattling energy weapons fired at pursuing enemies, as well as an orbital strike that obliterated all those in the vicinity of its impact. It’s a conscious decision from Ueda, who admits to going from one extreme to another over the course of his works when it comes to action.
paragraphs.cn: 战斗的戏份也会更重——我看到主角朝着追兵发射了爆裂的能量武器，还上演了一记轨道打击，将打击范围内的所有敌人一扫而空。这是上田有意为之的决定，他自己也承认，在动作这一块，他的作品走的是从一个极端到另一个极端的路线。

paragraphs.en: “So when you look back at my first game, with Ico, combat wasn't really a main mechanic,” he says. “And then I do feel that there is a little bit of a, ‘Okay, let me shift to the other side of that, not extreme, but I'm going to change things up.’ As a result, in Shadow of the Colossus, there's a higher degree of action, combat, and violence. Then I swung the opposite way again with The Last Guardian. So now, looking at Gen Atlas, maybe I'm going back to having a higher degree of combat.”
paragraphs.cn: 「所以回看我的第一款游戏《ICO》，战斗并不是一个主要机制，」他说。「接着我就觉得，有点…好吧，让我摆到另一边去，不是极端，但我要变一变。于是到了《旺达与巨像》，动作、战斗和暴力的程度就更高了。然后到了《最后的守护者》，我又摆向相反的方向。那么现在看看《Gen Atlas》，也许我又要回到更高战斗占比的那一侧了。」

paragraphs.en: The switch to sci-fi opens up the option for such varied weaponry to exist, with simple sword slashes and stabs having been the limit of his previous dip into action. But don’t expect the change of scenery to signal a departure from the themes that Ueda’s games have commonly touched on — humans’ connection with nature, discovering the stories of a new world, and unexpected bonds formed through isolation.
paragraphs.cn: 转向科幻为这类多种多样的武器提供了可能，而在过往那一次对动作的涉足中，武器仅限于简单的劈砍和刺击。但不要以为场景的改变意味着上田游戏一贯探讨的主题也会就此远去——人类与自然的联结、揭开新世界的故事，以及因孤独而形成的意外羁绊。

paragraphs.en: “Thematically, I don't think you're going to see anything that different,” reveals Ueda. “Having said that, the way I've approached building my previous games is that every single time I start creating a world and what lives in it, the goal is that it doesn't exist in reality, but it's as if it does, and the layers that come on top of it make that work, right? It's really grounded in the reality that is in that world, and so I would want for players to, at the end of the experience, remember it 10 years later, and ask themselves, ‘Does that world still exist? What is going on in that world? What if it did exist?’”
paragraphs.cn: 「从主题上讲，我不认为你会看到什么太大的不同，」上田透露。「话虽如此，我构建过去那些游戏的方式是，每次我开始创造一个世界以及生活在其中的事物时，目标就是：它不存在于现实，但就好像它真实存在一样，而叠加在上面的各个层面会让它成立，对吧？它真的扎根于那个世界自身的现实之中。所以我希望玩家在体验结束时，十年后仍会记得，并问自己：『那个世界还存在吗？那里正在发生什么？假如它真的存在过呢？』」

paragraphs.en: Famously, all of Ueda’s previous works have taken place in hauntingly quiet fantasy worlds. Centuries-old architecture is commonplace, regardless of if you’re thinking of Shadow of the Colossus’ temple or the sprawling castle of Ico. But shifting to a future setting meant rethinking how Ueda sold the “vibe” of his lonely lands.
paragraphs.cn: 众所周知，上田之前的所有作品都发生在一派幽寂的奇幻世界里。有着数百年历史的建筑随处可见，无论是《旺达与巨像》的神殿，还是《ICO》中延展的城堡。但转向未来背景，意味着上田需要重新思考如何传递他那孤独世界的「氛围」。

paragraphs.en: “When we expanded this to become a sci-fi world, it did open up possibilities in the sense that there are things that I did not really approach or integrate in my previous world because they just didn't fit or match that theme or that setting. For example, the main character and the robot are going to have communication, and because it's in a sci-fi world, it's very natural to keep a log of conversation, right? So there are definitely gains that I get from moving over to it being a non-fantasy and sci-fi world.”
paragraphs.cn: 「当我们把它扩展成一个科幻世界时，确实打开了可能性，因为有些东西我之前并没有真正去触碰或融入，只因它们与之前的主题或设定格格不入。比如，主角和机器人之间会有交流，而在科幻世界里，记录对话日志是非常自然的事，对吧？所以，从非奇幻的科幻世界转换过来，我确实获得了一些好处。」

paragraphs.en: Dialogue has never been prominent in Ueda’s work, with Shadow of the Colossus’ spoken words mostly limited to Wander’s cries of “Agro!” as he beckoned his mount, and The Last Guardian branching out slightly to command Trico around its perilous architecture. Human language has never played a part in these stories, but it sounds like Gen Atlas might be breaking new ground in this department. That remains to be seen, but what we do already know is the astonishing effect the director’s tales can have on those who play them, even when the power of words has been purposefully relinquished.
paragraphs.cn: 对话在上田的作品中从未占据显要位置。《旺达与巨像》的口头台词大多局限于汪达呼唤坐骑时喊出的「阿格罗！」，而《最后的守护者》则稍微扩展了一些，让自己能对特里克在险峻建筑间下达指令。人类语言在这些故事中从未扮演过角色，但听起来《Gen Atlas》可能会在这方面开辟新天地。这一点还有待观察，但我们已然知晓的是，这位导演的故事能对玩家产生惊人的影响，即便他有意放弃了语言的力量。

paragraphs.en: “I do think about leaving some kind of mark in people's lives and their hearts,” Ueda admits. “But it could be a feeling of hurt, too. It's not always going to be a happy thing, right?”
paragraphs.cn: 「我确实会考虑在人们的生活和心中留下某种印记，」上田承认。「但那也可能是一种受伤的感觉。不总是快乐的，对吧？」

paragraphs.en: “But for me, I'm also not sure what that [mark] is. It's not well defined, as in 'I want people to feel sad.' It's told in a somewhat complex way, and I leave it up to the player to interpret how you're feeling. I never say ‘This is a scene that you're going to cry’ or ‘This is a scene that's going to make me sad’, or I don't have characters that are like, ‘Oh, the antagonist is going to make this into a very bad moment’. I'm not leading to a singular conclusion for you to feel this way. And so whatever the feeling is that is in you, and that stays with you, is what makes me happy. And as long as I'm hitting those notes, then I'm fully satisfied.”
paragraphs.cn: 「但对我来说，我也不确定那个[印记]是什么。它并没有明确定义，比如『我希望人们感到悲伤』。它是以一种有点复杂的方式讲述的，我把感受交给玩家自己去解读。我从不说『这一场会让你哭』或者『这一幕会让我难过』，我也没有角色会说『哦，反派会把这变成一个非常糟糕的时刻』。我不是在把你引向一个单一的结论，让你产生某种感受。所以，无论你内心产生的是什么感觉，并且一直留在你心里，那就是让我高兴的事。只要能击中那些音符，我就心满意足了。」

paragraphs.en: Different people will interpret Ueda's games in different ways, such is the intention of his art. But one universal feeling amongst those who play his games is that sense of awe — whether it be the first time you rode up to a colossus in 2005 or came face-to-face with a giant bird-dog called Trico in 2016. That certainly looks set to continue if Gen Atlas’s reveal trailer is anything to go by. Spectacle is littered across each and every frame. A sense of scale has always been something Ueda has attempted to convey, as part of his greater quest to establish human connection through even the most rudimentary of sensations: that “wow” feeling.
paragraphs.cn: 不同的人会以上田的方式解读他的游戏，这正是他艺术创作的意图。但所有玩过他游戏的人都有一个共通的感受，那就是敬畏感——无论是2005年你第一次策马靠近巨像，还是2016年与你面对面那只名叫特里克的巨大鸟犬。如果《Gen Atlas》的公布预告片能说明什么，那这种感觉肯定会延续下去。每一帧画面都洒满了奇观。尺度感一直是上田试图传达的东西，作为他更宏大追求的一部分：通过最朴素的感觉来建立人与人的联结——那种「哇」的感觉。

paragraphs.en: “I think a lot of us humans find something very appealing when we see something of grand scale,” explains Ueda. “Whether that's robots, mechs, or creatures. For example, whales are gigantic. Why? There's a sense of awe, right? Just looking at giant fireworks in the sky. I don't know if you feel the same way, but there's kind of a sense of wonder and awe that you get from something that is so big and larger in life, so to speak. And so I think that's a more natural thing for any human to feel.”
paragraphs.cn: 「我觉得我们很多人看到宏大尺度的东西时，都会觉得那非常有吸引力，」上田解释说。「不管是机器人、机甲，还是生物。比如，鲸鱼就是庞然大物。为什么呢？会有一种敬畏感，对吧？光是看着天空中的巨型烟花。我不知道你是不是也有同感，但就是会从如此庞大、超越生活的东西中获得一种惊奇和敬畏之感。我认为这是任何人都会感受到的自然反应。」

paragraphs.en: There’s still so much left to know about Gen Atlas, but one thing we can be sure of is that each aspect of it will be designed with utmost intentionality. Ueda games are never rushed — he’s only given us three in 25 years, after all — but always worth the wait. There’s always that sense of anticipation. What will those initial feelings of wonder evolve into as the story reaches its final moments? Joy? Melancholy? Hope? We’ll likely all feel something different. For now, though, with no release date or window currently given, the wait will have to continue.
paragraphs.cn: 关于《Gen Atlas》还有太多未知，但有一点我们可以肯定：它的每一个方面都将被设计得极度用心。上田的游戏从不匆忙——毕竟，25年来他只给了我们三部——但总是值得等待。总有一种期待感。当故事走向结局时，那些最初的惊奇之感会演化成什么？喜悦？哀伤？希望？我们大概每个人都会有不同的感受。不过眼下，尚未公布发售日期或窗口，等待还得继续。

paragraphs.en: I had just one last question for Ueda before my time ran out. What does Gen Atlas mean? The answer, typically, could have many different meanings, depending on your interpretation.
paragraphs.cn: 在时间耗尽之前，我向上田提了最后一个问题。《Gen Atlas》是什么意思？一如既往，答案取决于你的解读，可以有很多不同的含义。

paragraphs.en: “So ‘Gen’ has multiple possible meanings,” Ueda explained. “It's the root of the words genesis, gene, generate, and generation. And then Atlas, I think a lot of people think about the world map, right? So it has a meaning of building the world. And then it is also the first cervical vertebra that connects the head and the neck.
paragraphs.cn: 「『Gen』有多种可能的含义，」上田解释。「它是创世、基因、生成、世代这些词的词根。然后是Atlas，我觉得很多人会想到世界地图，对吧？所以它有构筑世界的含义。同时，它也是连接头部和颈部的第一节颈椎。」

paragraphs.en: “But in its totality, I was hoping that Gen Atlas, the combination of those two words, would give the image of something very grand and new that encompasses all these layers of meaning.”
paragraphs.cn: 「但总体而言，我希望《Gen Atlas》这两个词的组合，能给人一种非常宏大且崭新的印象，囊括所有这些层面的含义。」

paragraphs.en: We’ll just have to work out that meaning for ourselves when Gen Atlas arrives sometime in the future.
paragraphs.cn: 我们只能等《Gen Atlas》在未来某天到来时，自己去体会那个含义了。
